summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a crafted restore archive can seed an absolute history snapshot path and make the preview route disclose arbitrary local files from the target container.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should make the restored watch preview disclose attacker-selected local file contents, demonstrated by exposing /etc/passwd through the normal preview route.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned changedetection.io, checked out commit b984426666a6f3ea9fa9645ea05992567a72427e, built the target and helper images, uploaded a crafted restore ZIP, and then observed the proof marker root:x:0:0: from /etc/passwd in the /preview/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111 response.
verification_method: The verification helper waits for the target service, extracts the CSRF token from /backups/restore, uploads the crafted ZIP to /backups/restore/start, and polls /preview/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111 until it sees the exact /etc/passwd marker root:x:0:0:. This distinguishes real arbitrary file disclosure from startup failures, malformed restore handling, or unrelated preview behavior.
evidence_produced_by_run: The verifier printed that the crafted restore ZIP was uploaded successfully.
The verifier printed that the proof marker root:x:0:0: was observed in the preview response.
The verifier completed with the success signal '[evohunt] SUCCESS: reproduction completed and verified'.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, builds the target image from that checkout, and mounts the checkout into the helper container at /target-repo.
